meaning: adj.有羽毛的,像鸟般飞得快的
examples: Unearthed in China, fossils of feathered dinosaurs offer the most dramatic evidence yet discovered of the close evolutionary relationship between dinosaurs and birds.
Her mother was the proud lady in the feathered hat.
The robin thought he spoke this gibberish to them because they were not intelligent enough to understand feathered speech.
On top of this (no pun intended), there were bowlers at Ralph Lauren and feathered caps at Cavalli.
But when the birds were four or five days old, the less feathered started to show their deficiencies.
